name: Invalidate User Sessions
description: Force-terminate all active sessions for compromised users across all applications and services

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body:
1. Revoke all refresh tokens and active sessions at the identity provider (SSO/IdP)
2. Invalidate sessions in each directly-authenticated application
3. Clear server-side session stores (Redis, database session tables, in-memory caches)
4. For OAuth/OIDC: revoke all access tokens and refresh tokens via token revocation endpoint
5. For SAML: invalidate assertions and notify service providers
6. Verify the user is forced to re-authenticate on next access attempt
7. Monitor for immediate re-authentication — if it occurs from a suspicious IP, the attacker may have credentials too (escalate to credential reset)

sections.title: Tools
sections.body:
- Azure AD: Revoke-AzureADUserAllRefreshToken or Graph API revokeSignInSessions
- Okta: DELETE /api/v1/users/{userId}/sessions, clear all tokens
- Google Workspace: Admin > Users > Security > Sign out user
- AWS: aws sts revoke-security-credentials, CloudTrail token revocation
- Redis: DEL session:{session_id} or FLUSHDB for full reset
- Custom apps: DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id = ?

sections.title: Escalation
sections.body: If the attacker re-authenticates after session invalidation, they likely have the user's credentials. Immediately escalate to credential reset (ER-007) and account lockout (CO-026).
